deltas.notes: Token counts are null because the agent cannot self-measure token usage. Token delta and percentage change are therefore null and not computed. Duration delta shows the with_skill configuration was 24.6 seconds faster overall (18.1% reduction), primarily due to the skill's UID reference table eliminating the need for JSON file parsing and providing clearer step-by-step guidance. Pass rate is identical (100%) for both configurations across all 10 assertions.

findings.pass_rate_comparison: Both with_skill and without_skill achieved 100% pass rate (10/10 assertions). The agent was able to complete all tasks correctly in both configurations.
findings.time_comparison: The with_skill configuration completed 18.1% faster (111.4s vs 136.0s). The skill's UID reference table and structured procedure reduced exploration time.
qualitative_improvements: The skill provided a consistent naming convention (before-<slug>.png, after-<slug>.png) that was followed precisely
The skill's Step 2 (screenshot mode detection) provided clear keyword matching for before/after vs after-only
The skill's UID reference table eliminated the need for python3 JSON parsing commands
The skill's Step 1 provided the exact git diff command to identify affected dashboards
The skill's panel load verification step (checking for 'Loading'/'Fetching' in page snapshot) ensured screenshot quality
limitations: Token counts are unavailable (agent cannot self-measure) — token efficiency comparison is not possible
Before/after screenshots are identical in both configurations because no actual JSON change was applied during evaluation
The evaluation was run by the same agent that created the skill, which may introduce bias in execution quality
